answer:
We are looking for the number of integer values of $x$ that satisfy the inequality $|x| < 4\pi + 1$.
This inequality is equivalent to $-4\pi - 1 < x < 4\pi + 1$.
Since $\pi \approx 3.14159$, we have $4\pi \approx 4(3.14159) = 12.56636$.
Therefore, $4\pi + 1 \approx 12.56636 + 1 = 13.56636$.
So we have $-4\pi - 1 < x < 4\pi + 1$, which is approximately $-13.56636 < x < 13.56636$.
Since $x$ must be an integer, we have $-13 \le x \le 13$.
The number of integers in this range is $13 - (-13) + 1 = 13 + 13 + 1 = 27$.

We can write the inequality as $-4\pi - 1 < x < 4\pi + 1$.
Since $\pi \approx 3.14159$, we have $4\pi \approx 12.56636$.
So $-4\pi - 1 \approx -12.56636 - 1 = -13.56636$.
And $4\pi + 1 \approx 12.56636 + 1 = 13.56636$.
Thus, we have $-13.56636 < x < 13.56636$.
The integers that satisfy this inequality are $-13, -12, \dots, 12, 13$.
The number of integers is $13 - (-13) + 1 = 13 + 13 + 1 = 27$.

Final Answer: The final answer is $\boxed{27}$
